B1.title: Compound-soliton formation mechanism + basin + Gardner relation
B1.research_question: Why does the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV (BKdV) system tend to relax locally to a compound-soliton structure from generic initial conditions? What is the basin of attraction (which IC families converge, which don't)? In what precise sense is the local compound soliton equivalent to a Gardner soliton, and how does the dynamical equivalence break down globally?
physics_anchoring: Empirical observation: from generic ICs (Gaussian, smooth-localized sech^2, multi-pulse), BKdV evolution produces LOCALLY a coherent (u, v) bound structure that propagates as a unit.

The local compound soliton appears to satisfy approximately u ≈ v^2/2 within its support — i.e., m = u - v^2/2 ≈ 0 *locally inside* the compound structure, even though global ||m||_L2 may be nonzero due to ambient radiation.
When m ≡ 0 the BKdV v-equation reduces algebraically to Gardner: v_t + 6 v v_x + (3/2) v^2 v_x + v_xxx = 0.
BKdV-S5/S7 (stage 1) showed the m=0 manifold is NOT a dynamically invariant set of BKdV; m_t|_{m=0} = (v-1)(6 v v_x + v_xxx) is nonzero for peak v>1.

Therefore: the compound soliton is a *local* attractor that resembles a Gardner soliton, while *global* solutions exit any naive m=0 ansatz. The mechanism question is how this LOCAL equivalence is dynamically established and sustained while the GLOBAL m=0 manifold is repelling.
key_observables_to_consider: Local m(x, t) inside vs outside the v-peak support
Phase speed of compound vs Gardner soliton prediction
Decay of ambient radiation away from the compound peak
Basin: which IC families produce a compound soliton at T=10 vs which produce dispersive fragmentation (per BKdV-S3 deep)
Functional locking: scatter plot of u vs v^2/2 inside compound support over time

B2.title: Bore-soliton interaction phase diagram
B2.research_question: In the BKdV bore-soliton encounter (Burgers bore on u, KdV-like soliton on v, propagating toward each other), the interaction produces qualitatively different outcomes depending on (bore amplitude, soliton amplitude, relative speed). What are the distinct outcome regimes? Where are the phase boundaries in parameter space? Are the transitions sharp or smooth?
physics_anchoring: Empirical observation: bore-soliton encounters can produce transmission (soliton passes through), reflection (soliton bounces back), fusion (soliton absorbed into bore), or destruction (soliton fragmented to radiation).
The phase boundaries between these outcomes are not theoretically known for BKdV.
Key parameters: bore amplitude u_L (with smooth tanh profile), soliton amplitude A (sech^2 profile on v), initial separation, and propagation horizon T.
Per BKdV-S6: bore in u requires explicit viscosity (e.g., nu=5e-2) to suppress Gibbs; without it, eval will be dominated by numerical artifact rather than physics.
key_observables_to_consider: Final v amplitude, position, and number of peaks after bore encounter
Bore amplitude post-encounter (does the bore survive intact?)
Phase shift of soliton relative to free propagation
Mass/momentum exchange between u and v sectors
Time-resolved v(x, t) trajectory near the encounter location
